test voice speaks Portuguese when PT-BR selected
features: per-language voice config + in-session language switch + sound cues
English / Português-BR language toggle

fixes: accept 32-bit float WAV so embedded STT works

features: request mic access from Settings
af_heart default voice + refresh config gate on leaving Settings
one-click voice mode via embedded sherpa-onnx (no Python)

fixes: serialize terminal input and add output resync

features: Crew mode — turn any session into a JARVIS-style orchestrator: assign worker sessions to it, dispatch long-running work with crew_dispatch, and let workers report back on their own instead of you polling them
Worker reports queue in the orchestrator's inbox and inject into its terminal when it's idle; a per-orchestrator 'always deliver' lock injects them immediately, and Deliver-now flushes on demand
Crew pane with a typed report inbox, drag-and-drop to assign or unassign workers (in and out of a crew), a detachable live worker panel with a steer input, and crew/supervisor badges in the sidebar
A 'crew (auto)' mindmap board mirrors each orchestrator's supervision tree, and watchdogs nudge the orchestrator when a worker goes quiet past its expected report time
improvements: Sessions created through the MCP tools are now filed under a task instead of appearing loose — create_session takes a task, and new list_tasks / create_task tools let an agent pick or make one
assign_session and list_sessions can resolve a session by name, not just by id

internal: release pipeline now embeds the synced changelog into the release tag so the app reports the correct version

features: check for updates from Settings → general: see when a new version is available, update with one click, and restart to apply it

fixes: clicking + to open a new repo now shows the recent-repos dropdown in place instead of opening it off-screen where it was invisible
the tab bar no longer scrolls vertically — it now only scrolls sideways, so tabs can't drift up or down out of view

imported VS Code themes now re-skin the whole app — picking or importing a theme (e.g. a red one) recolors the entire interface (sidebar, buttons, empty state, panels), not just the terminal and code editor. Choosing the Quant Dark or Quant Light preset now also reliably switches back from an imported theme

consistent light/dark theme switching across the whole IDE — switching themes now re-skins everything together instead of leaving the terminal, code editor, and diff viewer on a mismatched palette. Light mode is a clean, neutral Quant Light that matches the rest of the app (the old Solarized-tinted panes are gone), and your chosen accent color (emerald / iris / ocean) now also drives the editor cursor and text selection, not just the chrome

features: a brand-new look — Quant has been re-skinned top to bottom with the new Quant Design System: refreshed colors, typography, and iconography across every screen, and panels that now float with soft shadows and rounded corners for a lighter, more focused, Apple-like feel

a unified title bar on macOS — the window's traffic-light buttons are now inset into Quant's own top toolbar instead of sitting in a separate grey strip, so the whole top of the window is one clean bar you can still drag to move the window

session controls moved where you'd expect them — the Files / Terminal / Mindmap / Voice pane toggles now live in the session's own header next to its name and mic, instead of a separate row, and the duplicate top-bar icons were cleaned up

detach a file into its own panel — open a file and click the new detach button on its tab to pop it out into the right-hand dock as a floating, resizable, re-tileable panel beside your terminal and mindmap; reattach it to a tab anytime, and your unsaved edits come along either way

push-to-talk dictation — hold the mic button (next to the voice button) or press Cmd+Shift+Space and speak; your words are transcribed by the local Whisper backend and streamed into the session's input line as you talk. It never submits for you — review the text and press Enter yourself
two ways to talk — press and hold for as long as you speak, or tap Cmd+Shift+V to toggle recording on and off for longer dictation; both shortcuts are rebindable under Settings → Keybindings → Voice

live streaming with no garbled text — partial transcripts are committed word-by-word only once they're stable, so what lands in the input reads cleanly and is never retracted or duplicated mid-sentence

adopt claude sessions born outside quant — "new session" gains a "resume an existing session" option: pick from the untracked claude conversations found for the repo's directory (with first message and age) or paste a session id, and quant resumes that conversation instead of starting fresh
re-point or detach a session's conversation — right-click a stopped session and "change claude session id" to attach a different claude conversation, or detach it so the next start begins a fresh one
agents can adopt sessions too — create_session accepts a claudeSessionId, plus new list_adoptable_sessions and set_session_claude_id MCP tools
renaming a session now renames the claude conversation too — quant runs /rename inside the running session so claude's own session title stays in sync

a built-in file explorer, VSCode-style — a new files panel on the right (toggle it from the icon strip) shows the active session's working directory as a tree: browse folders, create files and folders, rename inline, and delete with a confirmation; the panel is per-session and resizable, just like the sessions sidebar on the left

files open as real tabs — click a file in the tree and it opens in the center tab bar right next to your sessions, with its own unsaved-changes dot; switch tabs freely (drafts are kept), save with Cmd/Ctrl+S, and closing a tab with unsaved edits asks before discarding
edit code with syntax highlighting — a CodeMirror editor that follows your theme (including imported VS Code themes) and loads language support on demand for over a hundred languages
markdown opens rendered — tables, task lists, highlighted code fences, and live mermaid diagrams, with a rendered/source toggle so you can edit and watch the preview update as you type

view images and html too — images get a zoomable viewer with dimensions and file size; html files preview live in a sandboxed frame (scripts run isolated from the app) with the same preview/source toggle; svg works as both image and editable source

agents can show you a file — a new files_open tool lets a session's agent open any file from its working directory as a tab and reveal it in the files panel tree, so 'take a look at this file' actually opens it for you
fixes: file access from the new explorer is locked to each session's working directory — path traversal and symlink escapes are rejected, and the existing diff file read/write endpoints got the same hardening

features: voice recording mode — for long, thought-out answers that don't fit in one breath: while a session is listening, tap 'rec' (or just tell it 'I'm going to give a long answer, start recording') and the mic stays open across your pauses instead of cutting you off after a moment of silence; tap stop or say 'stop recording' when you're done and the whole thing is sent as one message
see your words as you speak — the voice pane now shows a live transcript that fills in while you're recording, so you can watch what's being captured before you send it

agents can start recording for you: ask a session to record and it opens the mic in recording mode itself, then ends when you say 'stop recording' (or 'finish recording' / 'end recording') — handy for dictating a long instruction hands-free

the mindmap pane is now per-session — open it on the session you're working in without it popping open on every other tab and client; each session remembers its own open/closed state while you work, and everything starts closed on a fresh launch (board contents are unaffected and stay saved per session)

the agent persona is now editable in Settings → Claude → 'agent persona': customize the base system prompt every session gets (how it should behave, what to emphasize, how it uses the mindmap and Quant tools), with 'load default to edit' and 'reset to default' buttons; leave it empty to keep the built-in default, which still updates with new releases

voice mode — talk to a session hands-free and hear it talk back: open the new voice pane on a session (the 'voice' toggle in the header) and an audio-reactive orb visualizes the conversation while you speak naturally; silence detection handles turn-taking, and you can talk over a reply to interrupt it (barge-in)

voice runs fully local and private — your own Whisper (speech-to-text) and Kokoro (text-to-speech) engines, no cloud and no API key; Settings → Voice walks a new user through install with copy-paste cards per OS (native or Docker for Kokoro) and a 'Test connection' button for each engine

Settings → Voice: pick the voice, model and speaking speed from auto-discovered dropdowns (no guessing valid names), tune how long it waits for you to finish talking with a silence/pause slider, and edit the assistant's spoken persona/instructions; optional copy-paste scripts set the engines to auto-start on login (macOS/Linux/Windows)
agents can drive a spoken conversation through new MCP tools voice_listen, voice_speak and voice_converse, scoped to their own session

every session's agent now knows it's running inside Quant: a built-in persona teaches it what Quant is, how to draw its plan and progress on the live mindmap, and how to use the Quant tools — so it keeps you in the loop visually instead of just scrolling text (set QUANT_SKIP_PERSONA=1 to turn it off)

messages sent to another session over MCP (send_message) now submit on their own — an orchestrator agent can prompt a worker session and it runs immediately instead of waiting for you to press Enter; pass submit=false to only type the text

fixes: voice tools now work in the integrated terminal on macOS — the app declares microphone access so the system prompts for permission (previously you could hear audio play back but speech was never captured)

features: agents can now edit another session's mindmap over MCP: mindmap_set_node, mindmap_remove_node and mindmap_clear take an optional sessionId, so an orchestrator agent can draw on or tidy a worker session's board (omit it to keep editing your own)
open the mindmap full screen — a maximize button in the toolbar expands it to fill the window, Esc to exit
the mindmap pane now opens and closes everywhere at once: toggle it in one session and every tab (and any connected browser) follows, while the terminal pane stays per-session
agents can read another session's mindmap over MCP: mindmap_get takes an optional sessionId, and a new mindmap_list_boards tool lists which sessions have boards
remote access: reach quant from any browser, anywhere, via a Cloudflare quick tunnel guarded by a generated passcode — toggle it in Settings → remote access (off by default)
stop a running session straight from its right-click menu in the sidebar (also covers the Cmd+Shift+W action, which a browser can't intercept)
live per-session mindmap pane the agent and user can draw on — nodes with status/notes/progress plus sticky notes, beside the terminal
named mindmap boards listed under each session in the sidebar; click to open, drag a board onto another session to move it
rename a mindmap board and pick per-node colors
add a contract tab to the job editor for declaring typed inputs/outputs (issue #50 metadata pipeline)
